title: Do I Need UX Design Services or Just Web Design?
excerpt: The line between "web design" and "UX design" can feel blurry. This post helps you understand what UX design services actually involve, when they add real value to a project, and when a standard web design approach will meet your needs.
content:
You've been quoted for "web design" by one agency and "UX design services" by another. The prices are different. The processes sound different. But is there actually a meaningful difference for your project?

Here's how to think about it.

What Standard Web Design Typically Includes

A typical web design package usually covers:

  • Visual design of pages (layout, color, typography)

  • Responsive design for mobile and desktop

  • Basic site structure and navigation

  • Design of standard page templates


This is appropriate for most small business websites, brochure sites, and content-driven projects where the user's journey is relatively straightforward.

What UX Design Adds

UX design services go deeper into the strategic and research layer:

  • User research (interviews, surveys, behavioral analysis)

  • Persona development

  • User journey mapping

  • Information architecture (IA) planning

  • Low-fidelity wireframing and prototyping

  • Usability testing and iteration


UX design is particularly valuable for:

  • Web applications where users complete complex tasks (booking, ordering, managing accounts)
  • E-commerce platforms where conversion optimization is critical
  • SaaS products used frequently and requiring low friction
  • Redesign projects where you're trying to understand why your current site isn't converting

When You Probably Don't Need Full UX Services

For a simple marketing website with a homepage, services page, and contact form, a skilled web designer with good UX instincts will serve you well. Full UX services add cost and time that may not be justified for straightforward projects.

When You Definitely Do

If your product involves complex workflows, multiple user types, frequent use, or if conversion is a critical business metric, investing in UX services will pay off significantly.

Both Under One Roof
